question: Do certain genres cluster within specific nationalities or spread across multiple countries?

analysis.design_rationale: A heatmap visually quantifies the number of artworks per genre-nationality combination. By organizing genres as rows and nationalities as columns, the matrix instantly reveals genre concentration or dispersion across countries, as well as distinctive clustering patterns. This conventional chart provides a deep yet immediate summary of overlaps and exclusives, leveraging categorical matrix mapping for a dataset with many unique genres and nationalities.
analysis.target_insight: Which genres are distributed across multiple nationalities, and which are clustered within few or single nationalities.

chart_encodings.other_aesthetics: annotate matrix with counts; colorbar for intensity

transformations: Split any multi-genre values in df['genre'] by commas for correct genre mapping
Group by genre and nationality, count artworks to create matrix
Pivot table so genres are rows and nationalities are columns
data_preparation_steps: Explode df['genre'] so every row has one genre
Create pivot table: index=genre, columns=nationality, values=count of artworks (id)
suggestion.matplotlib_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
df = pd.read_csv('csv_c2_sec\20.csv')
df['genre'] = df['genre'].str.split(',')
df_exploded = df.explode('genre')
df_exploded['genre'] = df_exploded['genre'].str.strip()
pivot = pd.pivot_table(df_exploded, index='genre', columns='nationality', values='id', aggfunc='count', fill_value=0)
plt.figure(figsize=(14,10))
import matplotlib as mpl
cmap = mpl.colormaps['viridis']
im = plt.imshow(pivot.values, cmap=cmap, aspect='auto')
plt.colorbar(im,label='Number of Artworks')
plt.xticks(np.arange(pivot.shape[1]), pivot.columns, rotation=40, ha='right')
plt.yticks(np.arange(pivot.shape[0]), pivot.index)
for i in range(pivot.shape[0]):
    for j in range(pivot.shape[1]):
        plt.text(j, i, str(pivot.values[i,j]), ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color='white' if pivot.values[i,j] > np.max(pivot.values)/2 else 'black')
plt.title('Distribution of Genres Across Nationalities\n(Clustered vs. Spread)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
